evidence: SECTION: B Trees In 4 Minutes Intro
Today I'm going to introduce you to B-trees.
B-trees are similar to red black trees in that they are balanced search trees. Except unlike red black trees, which are binary nature, B-trees nodes may have numerous children, not just two.
B-trees are well suited for database systems. And are designed to handle a large volume of data.
The height of a B-trees can be much less than red black trees based on their branching factor, which is the number of children at each node.

extraction.name: B-Tree Search for 47 (Conceptual)
extraction.description: A conceptual demonstration of searching for key 47 in a B-Tree, emphasizing disk operations. The initial tree structure and its keys are not explicitly defined in the text, only implied.
